First assume that φ = c ψ. Then we have φ∧ψ = (cψ)∧ψ = c(ψ∧ψ) = 0.
Conversely, assume that φ∧ψ = 0, that is (a1dx1 + a2dx2)∧(b1dx1 + b2dx2) = 0.
Expanding, we get a1b1dx1∧dx1 + a1b2dx1∧dx2 + a2b1dx2∧dx1 + b1b2dx2∧dx2 = 0.
But dx1∧dx1 = dx2∧dx2 = 0, and dx2∧dx1 = - dx1∧dx2.
It follows that (a1b2 - a2b1) dx1∧dx2 = 0, whence a1b2 - a2b1 = 0.
Assume that b1 ( or b2 ) is not zero (if both were zero, the proof is trivial).
It follows that a2 = , and hence 
φ = a1dx1 + a2dx2 = a1dx1 +  dx2 =  (b1dx1 + b2dx2) =  ψ, that is 
φ = c ψ, where c = .

questions.prompt_html: Let [r, θ, z] be the cylindrical coordinates of a point in 3-space. Prove that rdr∧dθ∧dz = dx∧dy∧dz.
questions.prompt_text: Let [r, θ, z] be the cylindrical coordinates of a point in 3-space. Prove that rdr∧dθ∧dz = dx∧dy∧dz.
questions.solution_html:
Recall that x = r cos(θ), y = r sin(θ), z = z.
Now dx = (r cos(θ)) dr + (r cos(θ)) dθ + (r cos(θ)) dz 
Hence, dx = cos(θ) dr - rsin(θ) dθ ................. (1)
Similarly, dy = sin(θ) dr + rcos(θ) dθ ................. (2), and dz = dz .............. (3)
From (1), (2), and (3) we have 
dx∧dy∧dz = (cos(θ) dr - rsin(θ) dθ)∧(sin(θ) dr + rcos(θ) dθ)∧dz.
Expanding and noting that dr∧dr = dθ∧dθ = 0, and dr∧dθ = - dθ∧dr, we obtain,
dx∧dy∧dz = r (cos2(θ) + sin2(θ)) dr∧dθ∧dz. But cos2(θ) + sin2(θ) = 1, whence 
dx∧dy∧dz = r dr∧dθ∧dz.
questions.solution_text: Recall that x = r cos(θ), y = r sin(θ), z = z. Now dx = (∂/dr) (r cos(θ)) dr + (∂/dθ) (r cos(θ)) dθ + (∂/dz) (r cos(θ)) dz Hence, dx = cos(θ) dr - rsin(θ) dθ ................. (1) Similarly, dy = sin(θ) dr + rcos(θ) dθ ................. (2), and dz = dz .............. (3) From (1), (2), and (3) we have dx∧dy∧dz = (cos(θ) dr - rsin(θ) dθ)∧(sin(θ) dr + rcos(θ) dθ)∧dz. Expanding and noting that dr∧dr = dθ∧dθ = 0, and dr∧dθ = - dθ∧dr, we obtain, dx∧dy∧dz = r (cos 2 (θ) + sin 2 (θ)) dr∧dθ∧dz. But cos 2 (θ) + sin 2 (θ) = 1, whence dx∧dy∧dz = r dr∧dθ∧dz.

questions.prompt_html:
Let Φ = (2xy - z2) dx + (2yz + x2) dy + (y2 - 2zx) dz be a differential 1-form defined on a star-like domain D in R3. 
(a) Show that Φ is exact on D.

questions.prompt_text: Let Φ = (2xy - z 2 ) dx + (2yz + x 2 ) dy + (y 2 - 2zx) dz be a differential 1-form defined on a star-like domain D in R 3 . (a) Show that Φ is exact on D. (b) Find a differential 0-form Ψsuch that Φ = dΨ on D.
questions.solution_html:
(a) By Poincaré's Lemma, we need only verify that Φ is closed on D.
Indeed, dΦ = (2ydx + 2xdy - 2zdz)∧dx + (2xdx + 2zdy + 2ydz)∧dy + (-2zdx + 2ydy - 2xdz)dz
Expanding and noting that dx∧dx = dy∧dy = dz∧dz = {0}, and dx∧dy = -dy∧dx, etc., we obtain dΦ = {0}. Hence Φ is closed on D.
(b) Ψ = x2y - xz2 + y2z + C, where C is an arbitrary constant real number.
